title: No‑Fault PIP minimum
question_text: Under Florida’s No‑Fault Law, what is the minimum required Personal Injury Protection (PIP) coverage?
explanation: Every owner must carry at least $10.000 in PIP and $10.000 in Property Damage Liability (PDL). PIP covers your own injuries regardless of fault.\br[PT] Todo proprietário deve ter pelo menos $10.000 em PIP e $10.000 em PDL. O PIP cobre seus próprios ferimentos independentemente de culpa.

title: DUI record length
question_text: How long must a DUI conviction remain on your Florida driving record?
explanation: A DUI conviction stays on your record for 75 years. This affects insurance rates and future penalties.\br[PT] Uma condenação por DUI permanece em sua ficha por 75 anos. Isso afeta os seguros e penalidades futuras.

title: DUI fine .15 or higher
question_text: What is the minimum fine for a first DUI conviction if your BAC is .15 or higher?
explanation: For BAC .15+ or with a minor in the vehicle, the fine is not less than $1.000 and not more than $2.000.\br[PT] Para TAS .15+ ou com menor no veículo, a multa não é inferior a $1.000 nem superior a $2.000.

title: First refusal suspension
question_text: If you refuse to submit to a breath, blood, or urine test after a DUI arrest, your license is suspended for:
explanation: First refusal = 1‑year suspension. Second refusal = 18‑month suspension and a first‑degree misdemeanor.\br[PT] Primeira recusa = suspensão de 1 ano. Segunda recusa = suspensão de 18 meses e contravenção de primeiro grau.

title: Second refusal penalty
question_text: What is the penalty for a second refusal to take a DUI test?
explanation: A second refusal is more severe: 18 months suspension plus a criminal misdemeanor charge.\br[PT] A segunda recusa é mais severa: suspensão de 18 meses mais acusação criminal de contravenção.

title: DUI school hours first offense
question_text: After a DUI conviction, how many hours of DUI school are required for a first offense?
explanation: First offense requires 12 hours of DUI school. Second and third offenses require 21 hours.\br[PT] Primeira infração exige 12 horas de curso sobre DUI. Segunda e terceira exigem 21 horas.

title: Zero tolerance suspension
question_text: For a driver under 21, a BAC of .02 or higher results in:
explanation: Zero Tolerance law: any detectable alcohol (.02+) = 6‑month suspension. BAC .05+ also requires a substance abuse course.\br[PT] Lei Tolerância Zero: qualquer álcool detectável (.02+) = suspensão de 6 meses. TAS .05+ também exige curso sobre abuso de substâncias.

title: Texting points base
question_text: How many points will you receive for a first conviction of texting while driving?
explanation: Texting while driving = 3 points. Add 3 more points if it occurs in a school zone, and 6 points if it causes a crash.\br[PT] Dirigir digitando = 3 pontos. Adicione mais 3 pontos se ocorrer em zona escolar, e 6 pontos se causar acidente.

title: Second offense 50+ over fine
question_text: What is the maximum fine for a second offense of exceeding the speed limit by more than 50 mph?
explanation: First offense fine up to $1.000; second offense up to $2,500. Fines are doubled in school/work zones.\br[PT] Primeira infração multa até $1.000; segunda até $2.500. Multas dobram em zonas escolares/de obra.

title: 18 points suspension
question_text: If you accumulate 18 points within an 18‑month period, your license will be suspended for:
explanation: 12 points in 12 months = 30 days; 18 points in 18 months = 3 months; 24 points in 36 months = 1 year.\br[PT] 12 pontos em 12 meses = 30 dias; 18 pontos em 18 meses = 3 meses; 24 pontos em 36 meses = 1 ano.

title: Minor 6 points restriction
question_text: A driver under 18 who accumulates 6 or more points in 12 months is restricted to:
explanation: Mandatory restriction to "Business Purposes ONLY" for one year. Each additional point adds 90 days.\br[PT] Restrição obrigatória para "APENAS Finalidades Comerciais" por um ano. Cada ponto adicional adiciona 90 dias.

title: Tire tread depth
question_text: What is the minimum tread depth required for tires in Florida?
explanation: Tires must have at least 2/32 inch tread depth. The handbook recommends 3/32 inch for safety.\br[PT] Pneus devem ter no mínimo 2/32 polegada de profundidade. O manual recomenda 3/32 polegada por segurança.

title: Horn distance
question_text: How far must a driver’s horn be heard?
explanation: Your horn must be heard from a distance of 200 feet. Use it only for warning, not anger.\br[PT] Sua buzina deve ser ouvida a 200 pés. Use‑a apenas para aviso, não por raiva.

title: Bumper height cars 3500+
question_text: What is the maximum allowable bumper height for a passenger car weighing 3,500 lbs or more?
explanation: Bumper height depends on vehicle weight. For cars ≥3,500 lbs, front max 27″, rear max 29″.\br[PT] A altura do para-choque depende do peso do veículo. Para carros ≥3.500 lbs, frente máx 27″, traseira máx 29″.

title: Fog lights
question_text: When driving in fog you should use:
explanation: Low beams reduce glare and reflect less off water droplets. High beams worsen visibility.\br[PT] Faróis baixos reduzem ofuscamento e refletem menos nas gotículas de água. Faróis altos pioram a visibilidade.

title: Golf cart age after Oct 2023
question_text: What is the minimum age to operate a golf cart on a public road after October 1, 2023?
opt2: 16 with a valid learner’s or driver license
explanation: Under 18 must have a learner’s or driver license. 18+ must have a government‑issued photo ID.\br[PT] Menores de 18 devem ter Permissão para Aprendiz ou CNH. Maiores de 18 devem ter documento oficial com foto.

title: LSV speed limit
question_text: A low‑speed vehicle (LSV) may only be operated on roads with a posted speed limit of:
explanation: LSVs (top speed 21‑25 mph) are restricted to streets with a speed limit of 35 mph or less.\br[PT] VLV (velocidade máxima 21‑25 mph) são restritas a vias com limite de 35 mph ou menos.

title: Truck following distance
question_text: What is the minimum following distance for a truck towing another vehicle (except in cities)?
explanation: A truck or vehicle towing another must not follow within 300 feet of another truck or towed vehicle.\br[PT] Um caminhão ou veículo rebocando outro não pode seguir a menos de 300 pés de outro caminhão ou veículo rebocado.

title: Dark signal outage
question_text: If a traffic signal is completely dark (power outage), you must:
explanation: When signals are out, treat the intersection as a 4‑way stop. First to arrive goes first; if simultaneous, left yields to right.\br[PT] Quando os sinais estão apagados, trate o cruzamento como uma parada de 4 vias. O primeiro a chegar passa primeiro; se simultâneo, o da esquerda cede ao da direita.

title: Rural mailbox parking
question_text: A driver may not park within how many feet of a rural mailbox on a state highway between 8 am and 6 pm?
explanation: Parking within 30 feet of a rural mailbox is prohibited during those hours to allow mail delivery.\br[PT] Estacionar a menos de 30 pés de uma caixa de correio rural é proibido nesse horário para permitir a entrega de correspondência.

title: Litter over 15 pounds
question_text: What is the penalty for littering more than 15 pounds from a vehicle?
opt2: First‑degree misdemeanor, up to $1.000 fine and 3 points
explanation: More than 15 pounds = first‑degree misdemeanor, fine up to $1.000, 3 points, and possible litter pickup.\br[PT] Mais de 15 libras = contravenção de primeiro grau, multa até $1.000, 3 pontos, e possível coleta de lixo.

title: Child restraint driver responsibility
question_text: If you are the driver and a passenger under 18 is not wearing a safety belt, who receives the citation?
explanation: The driver is responsible for all passengers under 18. Passengers 18+ are cited individually.\br[PT] O motorista é responsável por todos os passageiros menores de 18. Passageiros com 18+ são multados individualmente.

title: School bus passing fine (children side)
question_text: What is the minimum fine for illegally passing a stopped school bus on the side where children enter/exit?
explanation: Minimum fine $465 plus 4 points and mandatory BDI course. $265 for passing on the opposite side.\br[PT] Multa mínima $465 mais 4 pontos e curso BDI obrigatório. $265 para ultrapassagem pelo lado oposto.

title: Crash report deadline
question_text: After a crash that results in death or serious injury, you must make a written report to FLHSMV within:
explanation: If no law enforcement report is made, you must file a written report to FLHSMV within 10 days.\br[PT] Se nenhum relatório policial for feito, você deve apresentar um relatório por escrito ao FLHSMV em até 10 dias.

title: Flashing yellow arrow
question_text: What does a flashing yellow arrow at a traffic light mean?
opt2: Turns allowed in direction of arrow, but yield to oncoming traffic
explanation: Flashing yellow arrow means you may turn, but oncoming traffic has a green light. You must yield.\br[PT] Seta amarela piscando significa que você pode virar, mas o tráfego em sentido oposto tem sinal verde. Você deve ceder passagem.

title: Red reflectors meaning
question_text: If you see red reflectors on lane lines facing you, what does that mean?
opt1: You are driving the wrong way
explanation: Red reflectors always face traffic going the wrong direction. Turn around immediately.\br[PT] Refletores vermelhos sempre ficam voltados para o tráfego que vai na direção errada. Vire imediatamente.

title: Learner’s license minimum age
question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a learner’s license in Florida?
explanation: You may apply at age 15 after completing the TLSAE course and passing vision/knowledge exams.\br[PT] Você pode solicitar aos 15 anos após concluir o curso TLSAE e passar nos exames de vista e conhecimento.

title: Required driving hours for full license
question_text: How many hours of supervised driving (including 10 at night) are required before a learner’s license holder can get a Class E license?
explanation: A parent or guardian must certify at least 50 hours, with 10 of those at night.\br[PT] Um pai ou responsável deve certificar pelo menos 50 horas, sendo 10 delas à noite.

title: First license fee
question_text: What is the initial fee for a first Florida driver license?
explanation: The initial license fee is $48. Learner’s license and renewals are also $48.\br[PT] A taxa inicial da licença é $48. A Permissão para Aprendiz e as renovações também são $48.

title: Non‑resident employment license deadline
question_text: A non‑resident who accepts employment in Florida must obtain a Florida driver license within:
explanation: Within 30 days after beginning employment or enrolling a child in a Florida public school.\br[PT] Dentro de 30 dias após iniciar o emprego ou matricular uma criança em uma escola pública da Flórida.

title: Driving test not required maneuver
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a required maneuver on the driving skills test?
explanation: The test includes backing up 50 feet, three‑point turn, straight‑in parking, stopping quickly, and obeying signs – but not parallel parking.\br[PT] O teste inclui ré por 50 pés, conversão em três tempos, estacionamento reto, parada rápida e obediência a placas – mas não estacionamento paralelo.

title: Backing up test restriction
question_text: On the driving skills test, when backing up you may NOT use:
opt2: Your rear‑view mirror or backup camera
explanation: The examiner requires you to look to the rear. Using the rear‑view mirror or backup camera is prohibited.\br[PT] O examinador exige que você olhe para trás. Usar o espelho retrovisor ou a câmera de ré é proibido.

title: Restriction Code B meaning
question_text: What restriction code means the driver must wear corrective lenses?
explanation: Restriction Code B = Corrective lenses. Code C = Hand controls. Code F = Outside mirror.\br[PT] Código de restrição B = Lentes corretivas. Código C = Controles manuais. Código F = Espelho externo.

title: Leaving scene points
question_text: How many points are added for leaving the scene of a crash resulting in property damage over $50?
explanation: Leaving the scene with property damage over $50 adds 6 points – same as a crash from speeding.\br[PT] Deixar o local com danos materiais acima de $50 adiciona 6 pontos – igual a um acidente por excesso de velocidade.

title: Sharrow meaning
question_text: What does a “sharrow” pavement marking indicate?
opt2: Shared lane – bicyclists may use full lane
explanation: Sharrows alert motorists that bicyclists may use the entire lane and indicate where cyclists should ride.\br[PT] Sharrows alertam os motoristas de que ciclistas podem usar toda a faixa e indicam onde os ciclistas devem andar.

title: Bike lane crossing for right turn
question_text: A motor vehicle may enter a bicycle lane:
opt2: Only for up to 200 feet before a right turn, after yielding to bicyclists
opt3: At any time if no bicyclist is present
explanation: You may cross a bike lane to turn right, but only when no bicyclists are present and you yield.\br[PT] Você pode cruzar uma ciclofaixa para virar à direita, mas somente quando não houver ciclistas e você ceder passagem.

title: Funeral procession right‑of‑way
question_text: When a funeral procession enters an intersection, the following vehicles may:
opt1: Follow through regardless of traffic signals
opt3: Only proceed if they have their own green light
explanation: Once the lead vehicle enters, all vehicles in the procession may follow through, even on red. Headlights or hazard lights must be on.\br[PT] Uma vez que o veículo líder entre, todos os veículos do cortejo podem continuar, mesmo no vermelho. Faróis ou pisca‑alertas devem estar ligados.

title: 50+ over first offense fine
question_text: What is the minimum fine for a first offense of exceeding the speed limit by more than 50 mph?
explanation: First offense fine up to $1.000; second offense up to $2,500. Fines are doubled in school/work zones.\br[PT] Primeira infração multa até $1.000; segunda até $2.500. Multas dobram em zonas escolares/de obra.

title: Driver 80+ renewal requirement
question_text: A driver 80 years or older renewing their license must:
opt2: Pass a vision exam (can be done by their own doctor)
explanation: Drivers 80+ must pass a vision exam. It can be done at a service center or by a licensed practitioner using Form HSMV 72119.\br[PT] Motoristas com 80+ devem passar por exame de vista. Pode ser feito em um centro de atendimento ou por um profissional licenciado usando o Formulário HSMV 72119.

title: DUI with minor fine
question_text: What is the maximum fine for a first DUI conviction with a minor in the vehicle?
explanation: If a minor is in the vehicle, the fine is not less than $1.000 and not more than $2.000 – same as BAC .15+.\br[PT] Se houver um menor no veículo, a multa não é inferior a $1.000 nem superior a $2.000 – igual a TAS .15+.

title: DUI refusal suspension
sentence: A driver who refuses to take a breath test after a DUI arrest will have their license suspended for ______ year(s).
example1: Refusal = automatic 1‑year suspension.\br[PT] Recusa = suspensão automática de 1 ano.

title: Zero tolerance BAC limit
sentence: For a driver under 21, any BAC of ______ or higher results in a six‑month suspension.
explanation: Zero Tolerance law.\br[PT] Lei Tolerância Zero.

title: DUI school hours first
sentence: A first DUI conviction requires ______ hours of DUI school.
explanation: First offense = 12 hours. Second or third = 21 hours.\br[PT] Primeira infração = 12 horas. Segunda ou terceira = 21 horas.

title: Texting school zone extra points
sentence: Texting while driving in a school zone adds an additional ______ points to the primary offense.
explanation: Texting = 3 points. School zone adds 3 more.\br[PT] Digitar = 3 pontos. Zona escolar adiciona mais 3.

title: Dark signal treatment
sentence: If a traffic light is completely dark, treat the intersection as a ______-way stop.
explanation: Power outage = all directions stop.\br[PT] Falta de energia = parada em todas as direções.

title: Minor 6 points restriction type
sentence: A driver under 18 who accumulates 6 or more points in 12 months is restricted to driving for ______ purposes only for one year.
explanation: “Business Purposes ONLY” restriction.\br[PT] Restrição “APENAS Finalidades Comerciais”.

title: School bus two‑lane stop direction
sentence: On a two‑lane road, when a school bus stops with red lights flashing, vehicles in ______ directions must stop.
explanation: Unless divided by a raised barrier, both directions stop.\br[PT] A menos que haja uma barreira elevada, as duas direções param.

title: Following distance seconds
sentence: The minimum following distance in normal conditions is ______ seconds.
explanation: Use the 4‑second rule.\br[PT] Use a regra dos 4 segundos.

title: LSV speed limit max
sentence: A low‑speed vehicle may only be operated on roads with a posted speed limit of ______ mph or less.
explanation: LSV top speed 21‑25 mph, restricted to 35 mph roads.\br[PT] VLV velocidade máxima 21‑25 mph, restrita a vias de 35 mph.

title: Parking uphill without curb
sentence: When parking uphill without a curb, turn your wheels to the ______.
explanation: So the car rolls off the road, not into traffic.\br[PT] Para que o carro role para fora da pista, não para o tráfego.

title: School zone sign shape
sentence: A ______-shaped sign indicates a school zone.
explanation: Pentagon = school zone; round = railroad; diamond = warning.\br[PT] Pentágono = zona escolar; redondo = ferrovia; losango = advertência.

title: Tire tread depth 32nds
sentence: A tire must have a minimum tread depth of ______/32 inch.
explanation: 2/32 inch is legal minimum; 3/32 recommended.\br[PT] 2/32 polegada é o mínimo legal; 3/32 recomendado.

title: DUI revocation minimum days
sentence: A first DUI conviction results in license revocation for a minimum of ______ days.
explanation: Minimum 180 days revocation.\br[PT] Revogação mínima de 180 dias.

title: First license fee dollars
sentence: The initial fee for a first Florida driver license is $______.
explanation: $48 for first license, learner’s, and renewals.\br[PT] $48 para primeira licença, permissão e renovações.

title: Non‑resident employment days
sentence: A non‑resident who accepts employment in Florida must obtain a Florida license within ______ days.
explanation: 30 days after starting work or enrolling a child.\br[PT] 30 dias após iniciar o trabalho ou matricular uma criança.

title: Backing up test forbidden mirror
sentence: On the driving skills test, when backing up you may not use the rear‑view mirror or ______ camera.
explanation: You must look directly to the rear.\br[PT] Você deve olhar diretamente para trás.

title: Restriction code B
sentence: Restriction Code B means the driver must wear ______ lenses.
explanation: Code B = corrective lenses (glasses or contacts).\br[PT] Código B = lentes corretivas (óculos ou contatos).

title: Leaving scene property damage points
sentence: Leaving the scene of a crash with property damage over $50 adds ______ points.
explanation: 6 points for leaving the scene.\br[PT] 6 pontos por deixar o local.

title: Driver 80+ renewal exam
sentence: A driver 80 years or older must pass a ______ exam to renew their license.
explanation: Vision exam required; can be done by their own doctor.\br[PT] Exame de vista obrigatório; pode ser feito pelo seu próprio médico.

title: Second offense 50+ over fine
sentence: The fine for a second offense of exceeding the speed limit by more than 50 mph is up to $______.
explanation: Second offense up to $2,500.\br[PT] Segunda infração até $2.500.

title: Move Over Law (HB 425 Expansion)
definition: The Move Over Law requires drivers to vacate the lane closest to a stopped emergency, service, tow truck, sanitation, utility, or disabled vehicle with warning lights, flares, or visibly present persons.
explanation: If you cannot move over safely on a multi‑lane road, slow down to 20 mph below the posted speed limit. On a two‑lane road with speed limit 20 mph or less, slow to 5 mph.
example1: Example: A disabled car on the shoulder has its hazard lights on. You must change lanes away from it or slow down.
example2: Example: A sanitation truck is stopped with amber lights flashing. Move over if possible.
example3: Example: On a two‑lane road with 65 mph limit, reduce speed to 45 mph if you cannot move over.
usage1: The law now also protects disabled vehicles with warning lights, flares, or people visibly present.\br[PT] A lei agora também protege veículos defeituosos com luzes de advertência, sinalizadores ou pessoas visivelmente presentes.

title: No‑Fault Law (PIP & PDL)
definition: Florida's No‑Fault Law requires every vehicle owner to carry Personal Injury Protection (PIP) and Property Damage Liability (PDL), each with minimum $10.000 coverage.
explanation: PIP pays for your own injuries regardless of fault. PDL pays for damage you cause to others' property.
example1: You must show proof of Florida insurance when registering a vehicle and carry the insurance ID card (or electronic proof) while driving.
example2: Example: You are hit by another driver. Your PIP covers your medical bills up to $10.000 even if you were at fault.
example3: Example: You back into a neighbor's fence. Your PDL pays for the damage up to $10.000.
usage1: If you fail to maintain insurance, your license and registration can be suspended for up to 3 years. Reinstatement fees range from $150 to $500.\br[PT] Se você não mantiver o seguro, sua licença e registro podem ser suspensos por até 3 anos. As taxas de reintegração variam de $150 a $500.

title: DUI Penalties & Zero Tolerance
definition: For first DUI conviction (BAC .08 or higher): fine $500‑$1.000, license revocation minimum 180 days, 50 hours community service, 12‑hour DUI school.
explanation: If BAC .15+ or minor in vehicle: fine $1.000‑$2.000, jail up to 9 months, ignition interlock for 6 months.
example1: Zero Tolerance: Drivers under 21 with BAC .02+ get 6‑month suspension. BAC .05+ requires substance abuse course.
example2: Example: An 18‑year‑old has two beers and blows .03. License suspended for 6 months.
example3: Example: A first‑time DUI with BAC .10 results in $500 fine, 180 days revocation, 50 hours community service, and 12 hours of DUI school.
usage1: A DUI conviction stays on your driving record for 75 years. Refusing a breath test = automatic 1‑year suspension.\br[PT] Uma condenação por DUI permanece em sua ficha de motorista por 75 anos. Recusar o teste do bafômetro = suspensão automática de 1 ano.

title: School Bus Passing Rules
definition: On a two‑lane road, all traffic in both directions must stop for a stopped school bus with red lights flashing and stop sign extended.
explanation: If the highway is divided by a raised barrier or unpaved median at least 5 feet wide, only traffic going the same direction must stop. Painted lines are NOT barriers.
example1: Penalties: Minimum fine $265 ($465 if passing on the side where children enter/exit), 4 points, and mandatory Basic Driver Improvement course.
example2: If passing results in serious injury or death: $1,500 fine, 6 points, 120 hours community service in a trauma center, license suspension minimum 1 year.
example3: Example: You are on a 4‑lane road with a grass median. A school bus stops on the opposite side. You do NOT need to stop.
usage1: Stop and remain stopped until the bus retracts its stop signal and children are clear. Never pass a stopped school bus.\br[PT] Pare e permaneça parado até o ônibus recolher seu sinal de parada e as crianças estiverem seguras. Nunca ultrapasse um ônibus escolar parado.

title: Implied Consent Law
definition: By signing a Florida driver license, you automatically consent to a breath, blood, or urine test if lawfully arrested for DUI.
explanation: Refusal results in automatic 1‑year license suspension (first refusal). Second refusal: 18‑month suspension and a first‑degree misdemeanor.
example1: In cases involving death or serious injury, blood may be drawn without consent.
example2: Example: An officer arrests you for DUI and asks for a breath test. If you refuse, your license is suspended for one year immediately.
example3: Example: A second refusal within a few years leads to 18 months suspension and possible jail time.
usage1: Refusing the test does NOT avoid DUI charges; it adds additional penalties.\br[PT] Recusar o teste NÃO evita as acusações de DUI; adiciona penalidades adicionais.

title: Right‑of‑Way at Intersections
definition: At a 4‑way stop, the first vehicle to arrive goes first. If two arrive at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.
explanation: At a two‑way stop, the vehicle on the through road has right‑of‑way. Turning vehicles must yield to vehicles going straight.
example1: Always yield to pedestrians and bicyclists in crosswalks. At an open intersection (no signs/signals), yield to vehicles already in the intersection and to vehicles on your right.
example2: Example: You and another car arrive at a 4‑way stop simultaneously. You are on the left. You must yield to the car on your right.
example3: Example: You want to turn left at a two‑way stop. A car coming from the opposite direction is going straight. You must yield.
usage1: Even if you have right‑of‑way, you must do everything possible to avoid a crash. Do not assume other drivers will yield.\br[PT] Mesmo que você tenha o direito de passagem, você deve fazer todo o possível para evitar um acidente. Não presuma que os outros motoristas vão ceder.

title: Following Distance (4‑Second Rule)
definition: Keep a minimum following distance of four seconds under normal conditions.
explanation: Method: Pick a stationary marker (sign, pole). When the rear of the vehicle ahead passes it, count “one‑thousand‑one, one‑thousand‑two, one‑thousand‑three, one‑thousand‑four.” If you reach the marker before finishing, you are too close.
example1: Increase following distance in rain, fog, heavy traffic, when towing a trailer, or when following motorcycles and large trucks.
example2: Example: You are driving at 50 mph on a dry highway. Count four seconds to the vehicle ahead – that gives you about 268 feet of stopping distance.
example3: Example: It starts raining heavily. Increase your following distance to six or more seconds.
usage1: Rear‑end collisions are almost always caused by following too closely. The 4‑second rule works at any speed.\br[PT] Colisões traseiras são quase sempre causadas por seguir muito próximo. A regra dos 4 segundos funciona em qualquer velocidade.

title: Point System Suspension Thresholds
definition: 12 points in 12 months = 30‑day suspension; 18 points in 18 months = 3‑month suspension; 24 points in 36 months = 1‑year suspension.
explanation: Points are assigned for moving violations (speeding, red light, etc.). Out‑of‑state convictions add the same points.
example1: Example: A driver gets 6 points for a crash, then 4 points for running a red light, then 3 points for speeding – total 13 points in 10 months → 30‑day suspension.
example2: Example: A driver under 18 with 6 points is restricted to business purposes only for one year.
usage1: Points remain on your record for at least two years. Accumulating points can also increase insurance rates.\br[PT] Os pontos permanecem em seu registro por pelo menos dois anos. O acúmulo de pontos também pode aumentar as taxas de seguro.

title: DUI Penalties – Second Offense
definition: Fine $1.000‑$2.000 ($2.000‑$4.000 if BAC .15+ or minor in vehicle), jail up to 9 months, license revocation minimum 180 days (5 years if second within 5 years), 21‑hour DUI school, ignition interlock at least 1 year.
explanation: Second offense within 5 years triggers a mandatory 5‑year revocation.
example1: Example: A second DUI within 5 years: 5‑year revocation, jail up to 9 months, and interlock.
usage1: A DUI conviction stays on your record for 75 years. Even after revocation ends, you must install an ignition interlock device.\br[PT] Uma condenação por DUI permanece em seu registro por 75 anos. Mesmo após o fim da revogação, você deve instalar um dispositivo de trava de ignição.

title: Financial Responsibility Law after DUI
definition: After a DUI conviction, you must carry higher insurance limits: $100.000 Bodily Injury Liability per person, $300.000 per crash, and $50.000 PDL for 3 years.
explanation: These higher limits apply regardless of fault in a future crash.
example1: Example: You cause a crash with injuries after a DUI. Your insurance must cover at least $100.000 per injured person.
usage1: Failure to provide proof of these higher limits results in suspension of your driver license and vehicle registration for up to 3 years.\br[PT] A não apresentação de comprovante desses limites mais altos resulta na suspensão de sua carteira de motorista e registro do veículo por até 3 anos.

title: Equipment Standards – Windshields and Windows
definition: Windshield must be safety glass, no stickers except required by law. Side windows may not reflect more than 25% of light; rear window not more than 35%. No mirrored appearance.
explanation: Tinted windows that look like a mirror are illegal.
example1: Example: Aftermarket tint that gives a chrome or mirrored finish is prohibited.
usage1: Law enforcement may stop a vehicle solely for window tint violations. Medical exemptions are available but require documentation.\br[PT] A polícia pode parar um veículo apenas por violações de película. Isenções médicas estão disponíveis, mas exigem documentação.

title: ABS vs Conventional Brakes
definition: ABS (Anti‑lock Brake System): press hard, hold steady, and steer. Do not pump. Conventional brakes: pump the brake pedal rapidly to avoid lock‑up.
explanation: In an emergency stop, an ABS driver keeps constant pressure; a conventional driver pumps.
example1: Example: You have ABS and need to stop suddenly on a wet road. Press the brake firmly and keep pressing – you will feel a pulsing. That is normal.
example2: Example: Without ABS, pump the brake pedal about three times per second.
usage1: Check your owner’s manual or look for the ABS light on the dashboard (lights up briefly when you start the engine) to know which system you have.\br[PT] Verifique o manual do proprietário ou procure a luz ABS no painel (acende brevemente ao dar partida) para saber qual sistema você tem.

title: Parking on Hills – Complete Rules
definition: Downhill with curb → turn wheels toward curb. Uphill with curb → turn wheels away from curb. Uphill without curb → turn wheels to the right. Set parking brake.
explanation: Automatic transmission: shift to Park. Manual: Reverse (downhill) or First (uphill).
example1: Example: Downhill with curb: wheels right (toward curb).
example2: Example: Uphill without curb: wheels right so car rolls off road.
usage1: Always take the keys out of the vehicle before leaving. Florida law requires it.\br[PT] Sempre retire as chaves do veículo antes de sair. A lei da Flórida exige isso.

title: School Bus Stop Law – Divided Highways
definition: On a highway divided by a raised barrier or unpaved median at least 5 feet wide, only traffic going the same direction must stop. Painted lines are NOT barriers.
explanation: If there is only a painted median or double yellow lines, all traffic must stop.
example1: Example: A four‑lane road with a grass median. Bus stops on opposite side → you do not stop.
example2: Example: A road with a raised concrete barrier. You are going the same direction as the bus – you must stop.
usage1: Even when not required to stop, always be alert. Children may run across unexpectedly.\br[PT] Mesmo quando não for obrigatório parar, mantenha a atenção. As crianças podem atravessar inesperadamente.

title: Following a Fire Truck
definition: It is illegal to follow a fire truck responding to an emergency closer than 500 feet.
explanation: This also applies to any emergency vehicle (ambulance, police) with activated lights.
example1: Example: You see a fire engine with lights and sirens. Stay at least 500 feet behind.
usage1: The same 500‑foot rule applies when following any emergency vehicle. Violation can result in a citation and points.\br[PT] A mesma regra de 500 pés se aplica ao seguir qualquer veículo de emergência. A violação pode resultar em multa e pontos.

title: Three Crashes in Three Years Law
definition: If you are convicted of a third traffic offense causing a crash within 36 months, you must complete a 12‑hour Advanced Driver Improvement course, 4 hours of behind‑the‑wheel training, and pass an extended driving test.
explanation: The course must be FLHSMV‑approved.
example1: Example: Three at‑fault crashes in 3 years → mandatory ADI course and retesting.
usage1: Failure to complete the requirements within 90 days cancels your driver license.\br[PT] O não cumprimento dos requisitos em até 90 dias cancela sua carteira de motorista.

title: Hand Signals for Turning
definition: Left turn: left arm straight out. Right turn: left arm up (or bicyclists may use right arm out). Stop/slow: left arm down.
explanation: Hand signals are required when your vehicle’s turn signals or brake lights are not working.
example1: Example: Your turn signals fail. You use left arm straight out to indicate a left turn.
example2: Example: You need to slow down suddenly but brake lights are out – left arm down.
usage1: Bicyclists may signal a right turn by extending the right arm horizontally. This is an alternative to the left‑arm‑up signal.\br[PT] Ciclistas podem sinalizar uma conversão à direita estendendo o braço direito horizontalmente. Esta é uma alternativa ao sinal de braço esquerdo para cima.

title: HOV Lane Rules
definition: High Occupancy Vehicle lanes require 2+ occupants or a low‑emission vehicle decal. Enter and exit only at designated points. Diamond symbols mark the lane.
explanation: LEVs (low‑emission vehicles) need FLHSMV Form 83027 and a decal to use HOV lanes alone.
example1: Example: You drive alone in an HOV lane without a decal → citation.
usage1: HOV lanes are usually separated by a striped buffer zone. Crossing the buffer outside of designated points is illegal.\br[PT] Faixas HOV geralmente são separadas por uma zona de amortecimento listrada. Atravessar o buffer fora dos pontos designados é ilegal.

title: Red Reflectors on Lane Lines
definition: Red reflectors always mean you are facing traffic the wrong way. If you see them, turn around immediately.
explanation: Red reflectors are placed on lane lines to warn drivers going the wrong direction.
example1: Example: You enter an exit ramp and see red reflectors on the edge line → you are going the wrong way. Pull over and turn around.
usage1: If you see red reflectors on the edge lines of a ramp, you are on the wrong side. Do not continue – back up or turn around if safe.\br[PT] Se você vir refletores vermelhos nas linhas de borda de uma rampa, você está no lado errado. Não continue – dê ré ou vire se for seguro.

title: Roundabout Rules
definition: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout. Drive counter‑clockwise. Signal before exiting. Do not change lanes inside.
explanation: Choose lane before entering: right lane for right turns or straight; left lane for left turns or U‑turns.
example1: Example: You want to go straight. Use the right lane. Signal right when exiting.
example2: Example: You want to make a U‑turn. Use the left lane and stay in the roundabout until you exit.
usage1: If you miss your exit, do not stop or back up. Continue around the roundabout again.\br[PT] Se você perder sua saída, não pare nem dê ré. Continue ao redor da rotatória novamente.

title: Emergency Braking with ABS
definition: With ABS, press the brake pedal firmly and hold it. Do not pump. Steer around obstacles. The system will pump automatically many times per second.
explanation: You will feel a pulsing in the pedal. That is normal.
example1: Example: A deer jumps out. You brake hard and hold – ABS lets you steer while braking.
usage1: Do not remove pressure from the pedal or pump the brakes; that will disengage the ABS and increase stopping distance.\br[PT] Não remova a pressão do pedal nem bombeie os freios; isso desativará o ABS e aumentará a distância de parada.

title: Tire Blowout Procedure
definition: Take your foot off the gas. Do not brake. Grip the wheel firmly. Allow the vehicle to slow gradually. Brake softly when under control. Pull completely off the roadway.
explanation: A blowout often feels like a sudden pull to one side. Stay calm.
example1: Example: A loud bang and the car pulls right. You ease off gas, hold the wheel straight, coast to a stop.
usage1: Never slam on brakes – it can cause a rollover, especially in SUVs and trucks.\br[PT] Nunca pise fundo no freio – isso pode causar um capotamento, especialmente em SUVs e caminhões.

title: Carbon Monoxide Warning
definition: Running a car in a closed garage is deadly. Carbon monoxide is odorless and colorless. Even with the garage door open, it can accumulate.
explanation: Symptoms of CO poisoning: headache, dizziness, nausea, confusion.
example1: Example: Warming up your car in the garage with the door closed → risk of fatal poisoning.
usage1: Install a carbon monoxide detector in your home, especially near attached garages. Always pull the car outside before idling.\br[PT] Instale um detector de monóxido de carbono em sua casa, especialmente perto de garagens anexas. Sempre coloque o carro para fora antes de deixá‑lo ligado.

title: Drowsy Driving – Micro‑sleeps
definition: Fatigue slows reaction time and judgment. Micro‑sleeps are brief, involuntary episodes of sleep lasting a few seconds.
explanation: At 60 mph, a 4‑second micro‑sleep means you travel 352 feet without being conscious.
example1: Example: You catch yourself nodding off while driving → pull over and take a 20‑minute nap.
usage1: The two main causes of drowsy driving are lack of quality/quantity of sleep and driving during normal sleeping hours. Take a break every 100 miles or 2 hours.\br[PT] As duas principais causas da sonolência ao volante são a falta de qualidade/quantidade de sono e dirigir nos horários normais de sono. Faça uma pausa a cada 100 milhas ou 2 horas.

title: Driving Under the Influence of Drugs
definition: Driving while impaired by any drug – legal prescription, over‑the‑counter, or illegal – is DUI and carries the same penalties as alcohol.
explanation: Many medications warn against driving. Check the label.
example1: Example: Taking cold medication that causes drowsiness and then driving → DUI arrest possible.
example2: Example: Mixing allergy medicine with alcohol can multiply the effects, making one drink feel like several.
usage1: Always ask your doctor or pharmacist if a new medication will affect your ability to drive.\br[PT] Sempre pergunte ao seu médico ou farmacêutico se um novo medicamento afetará sua capacidade de dirigir.

title: Littering Penalties
definition: Up to 15 pounds: $100 fine. More than 15 pounds: first‑degree misdemeanor, fine up to $1.000, 3 points on driving record, and possible court‑ordered litter pickup.
explanation: Example: Throwing a bag of trash (20 lbs) from your car → $1.000 fine, 3 points.
usage1: Even a cigarette butt is litter and can be fined. The court may also order you to pick up litter along roadways.\br[PT] Até uma bituca de cigarro é lixo e pode ser multada. O tribunal também pode ordenar que você recolha lixo ao longo das estradas.

title: Basic Driver Improvement Course (BDI)
definition: You may elect to take a BDI course in lieu of points for a non‑criminal moving violation (except speeding 30+ mph over). You also get an 18% reduction in the civil penalty.
explanation: Example: You get a ticket for running a red light. You choose BDI → no points on your record.
usage1: You may make this election only once every 12 months, and no more than 5 times in your lifetime. You must inform the clerk of court within 30 days of the citation.\br[PT] Você pode fazer essa escolha apenas uma vez a cada 12 meses, e no máximo 5 vezes durante a vida. Você deve informar o escrivão do tribunal em até 30 dias da multa.

title: Advanced Driver Improvement (ADI) Course
definition: Required if your license is suspended for points, as a habitual traffic offender (non‑DUI), or by court order. Also may be required after a DUI in some cases.
explanation: Example: Your license is suspended after 18 points in 18 months. You must complete ADI to reinstate.
usage1: ADI is a 12‑hour course, more intensive than BDI. It includes group discussions, video, and often a final exam.\br[PT] ADI é um curso de 12 horas, mais intensivo que o BDI. Inclui discussões em grupo, vídeo e frequentemente um exame final.

title: Vision Standards for Licensing
definition: 20/40 or better in each eye passes without referral. 20/50 or worse requires eye specialist referral. Minimum field of vision is 130 degrees.
explanation: Example: You have 20/50 in one eye. You must see an eye specialist before a license is issued.
usage1: If you need corrective lenses to pass, a “B” restriction will be added. You must wear glasses or contacts every time you drive.\br[PT] Se você precisar de lentes corretivas para passar, uma restrição “B” será adicionada. Você deve usar óculos ou lentes de contato toda vez que dirigir.

title: Driver License Renewal for Non‑Immigrants
definition: Non‑immigrants must renew in person at a driver license service center. They cannot renew online. Expiration dates may vary and usually align with their I‑94 or visa expiration.
explanation: Example: You have a J‑1 visa. Your license expires – you must go to a service center with your immigration documents.
usage1: Check your I‑94 admission record before visiting. Your license cannot be issued beyond your authorized stay.\br[PT] Verifique seu registro de admissão I‑94 antes de visitar. Sua licença não pode ser emitida além do período de permanência autorizado.

title: Seat Belt Exemptions
definition: Exemptions: physician‑certified medical condition, newspaper home delivery employees while delivering, school buses purchased before 12/31/2000, farm equipment, trucks over 26.000 lbs.
explanation: Example: A delivery driver for a newspaper does not need to wear a belt while throwing papers.
usage1: If you have a medical exemption, keep the certification in your vehicle. Law enforcement may ask for it.\br[PT] Se você tiver uma isenção médica, mantenha a certificação em seu veículo. A polícia pode solicitá‑la.

title: Child Car Seat Requirements
definition: Under 4 years → separate child safety seat. Ages 4‑5 → safety seat or booster seat. Children under 13 should be in the rear seat. Never place a rear‑facing seat in front of an airbag.
explanation: Example: A 3‑year‑old must be in a child safety seat, not just a booster.
usage1: The driver is charged if any passenger under 18 is not properly restrained. This includes children in car seats.\br[PT] O motorista é multado se algum passageiro menor de 18 não estiver devidamente preso. Isso inclui crianças em cadeirinhas.

title: When You May Pass on the Right
definition: Passing on the right is legal only when there are two or more lanes moving in the same direction, or when the vehicle ahead is making a left turn and you have a paved lane to pass.
explanation: Example: On a 4‑lane highway, a car in the left lane is turning left. You may pass on the right in the right lane.
usage1: Never pass on the right on the shoulder – it is illegal and dangerous. Also never pass a vehicle that is already in a right‑turn lane.\br[PT] Nunca ultrapasse pela direita no acostamento – é ilegal e perigoso. Também nunca ultrapasse um veículo que já está em uma faixa de conversão à direita.

title: Drawbridge Signals
definition: Red light → stop at marked line (bridge is open). Yellow light → about to turn red, stop if safe. Flashing yellow → bridge in operation, slow down and prepare to stop. Green → proceed with caution.
explanation: Example: You approach a drawbridge and see a flashing yellow light → slow down and be ready to stop.
usage1: Never drive around or under a gate – it is illegal and extremely dangerous. The bridge may open at any time.\br[PT] Nunca dirija ao redor ou por baixo de uma cancela – é ilegal e extremamente perigoso. A ponte pode abrir a qualquer momento.

title: Pedestrian Hybrid Beacon
definition: Dark → go if clear. Flashing yellow → pedestrian has pushed button, slow down. Steady yellow → prepare to stop. Steady red → stop for pedestrian. Flashing red → proceed with caution if no pedestrian.
explanation: Example: You see a flashing yellow beacon. A pedestrian may be waiting. Slow down.
usage1: These beacons are often at mid‑block crosswalks on high‑speed roads. Always be prepared to stop.\br[PT] Esses faróis geralmente estão em travessias de pedestres no meio do quarteirão em estradas de alta velocidade. Sempre esteja preparado para parar.

title: School Zone Speed Detection Cameras (HB 657)
definition: Counties or municipalities may install camera‑enforced speed limit devices in school zones. The registered owner is subject to a $100 fine. Signs must warn drivers of camera enforcement.
explanation: Example: You drive 35 mph in a 20 mph school zone with camera signs → $100 ticket in the mail.
usage1: Fines are doubled for speeding in school zones even without cameras. Camera‑enforced fines are not doubled but still $100.\br[PT] As multas são duplicadas por excesso de velocidade em zonas escolares, mesmo sem câmeras. As multas com câmeras não são duplicadas, mas ainda são $100.

title: Golf Cart Operation (HB 949)
definition: Under 18 → must have a learner’s or driver license. 18+ → must have a government‑issued photo ID. Only allowed on designated roads. Unless local ordinance allows, sunrise to sunset only.
explanation: Example: A 17‑year‑old with a learner’s license may drive a golf cart on a designated road.
usage1: Child safety seats should not be used on golf carts – they are not crash‑tested for LSVs. Always obey all traffic laws.\br[PT] Cadeirinhas não devem ser usadas em carrinhos de golfe – elas não são testadas para VLV. Sempre obedeça a todas as leis de trânsito.

title: Motorcycle Helmet Law
definition: Riders under 21 must wear a helmet. Riders 21+ may ride without a helmet only if they carry at least $10.000 in medical insurance benefits.
explanation: Example: A 25‑year‑old rider with no insurance is stopped without a helmet → citation.
usage1: Even if legal, helmets save lives. The $10.000 medical insurance must specifically cover motorcycle‑related injuries.\br[PT] Mesmo que seja legal, os capacetes salvam vidas. O seguro médico de $10.000 deve cobrir especificamente lesões relacionadas a motocicleta.

title: Bicycle Lighting Requirements
definition: Between sunset and sunrise: front white light visible from 500 feet, rear red reflector and red light visible from 600 feet.
explanation: Example: You ride your bike at night with only a front light → illegal. You need rear red light.
usage1: Also wear reflective clothing. Helmets required for riders and passengers under 16. A white light on the front and red light on the rear are mandatory.\br[PT] Use também roupas reflexivas. Capacete obrigatório para condutores e passageiros menores de 16. Luz branca na frente e luz vermelha atrás são obrigatórias.

title: Commercial Vehicle “No Zones”
definition: Large blind spots in front, rear, and both sides of trucks and buses. If you cannot see the driver’s face in their mirror, they cannot see you.
explanation: Example: You are driving directly behind a semi‑trailer. The driver cannot see you in their mirrors – you are in the rear No Zone.
usage1: Pass quickly on the left side and avoid lingering. Never cut in front of a truck – they need extra stopping distance.\br[PT] Ultrapasse rapidamente pelo lado esquerdo e evite permanecer na zona cega. Nunca entre na frente de um caminhão – eles precisam de distância extra de parada.

title: Emergency Notification System (ENS) at Railroad Crossings
definition: Every highway‑rail grade crossing has a blue sign with an emergency number and crossing ID. If something is blocking the tracks or the signals malfunction, call that number.
explanation: Example: Your car stalls on the tracks. After getting everyone out, call the number on the blue sign.
usage1: Provide the crossing ID number to responders. Do not call 911 for a non‑emergency obstruction; use the ENS number.\br[PT] Forneça o número de identificação da passagem aos atendentes. Não ligue para 911 para uma obstrução não emergencial; use o número do ENS.

title: Submerged Vehicle Escape
definition: A vehicle floats for only 30‑60 seconds. Unbuckle your belt. Roll down the window before the car sinks. If you cannot, kick out a side window. Do not call 911 until you are out.
explanation: Example: Your car goes into a pond. You open the window and climb out before it sinks.
usage1: Keep an escape tool (window breaker) within reach, such as taped to the sun visor or in the center console.\br[PT] Mantenha uma ferramenta de escape (quebra‑vidro) ao alcance, como presa ao para‑sol ou no console central.

title: Right Wheels Off Pavement Recovery
definition: Take foot off gas. Hold the wheel firmly. Brake lightly. Wait until the road is clear, then turn back onto the pavement at a slow speed, signaling.
explanation: Example: Your right wheels drop onto the gravel shoulder. You ease off gas, hold wheel straight, then gently steer back on.
usage1: Do not jerk the wheel – you could overcorrect and crash. Overcorrection is a leading cause of rollovers.\br[PT] Não puxe o volante bruscamente – você pode corrigir em excesso e capotar. A correção excessiva é uma das principais causas de capotamentos.

title: Wet Brakes After Driving Through Water
definition: After driving through deep water, brakes may become wet and pull to one side or not hold. Dry them by driving slowly in low gear and gently applying the brakes.
explanation: Example: You go through a puddle. When you brake, the car pulls right. Drive slowly and tap the brakes until they work normally.
usage1: Never drive through flooded roads. Six inches of water can cause loss of control; one foot can float many vehicles. Turn around, don’t drown.\br[PT] Nunca dirija em estradas alagadas. Seis polegadas de água podem causar perda de controle; um pé pode flutuar muitos veículos. Vire, não se afogue.
